query: html id vs class interview question

competitors.label: HTML id global attribute
competitors.selectionReason: This is the canonical MDN reference for the id global attribute and the clearest official starting point for uniqueness, fragment targeting, and selector behavior.
theirStrengths: MDN is authoritative on uniqueness rules, fragment identifiers, and the DOM or CSS behavior tied to the id attribute.
theirGaps: The single-attribute reference is less explicit about the production trade-off between unique ids and reusable classes across real UI systems.

realWorldUseCases: id vs class is not just selector syntax: it affects fragment links, CSS specificity, JS hooks, and the common mistake of duplicating ids across reusable UI.
followUpConfusion: In production, the common mistake is treating both as generic labels: id is a unique identity hook for one node, while class is a reusable grouping hook for styling, behavior, fragment links, and test selectors.

realWorldUseCases: MDN defines id semantics clearly, but it is lighter on the broader production comparison between unique identity hooks and reusable grouping hooks.
actionableExamples: It includes direct id examples and fragment behavior, so the example criterion stays competitive.
followUpConfusion: Our page is more explicit about the follow-up confusion teams hit in practice: duplicate ids in reusable UI and overusing ids where classes should group styles or behavior.

actionableExamples: Keep the uniqueness-versus-reuse framing near the opening so the page stays faster to scan than two separate attribute references.

competitors.winReason: MDN is the canonical id reference, but our page is stronger as an interview answer because it turns id-versus-class into a concrete uniqueness, specificity, fragment-link, and reusable-UI decision.
